title: fagus sylvatica f. zlatia
intro: Fagus sylvatica f. zlatia, also known as the Copper Beech, is a cultivar of the European Beech tree, characterized by its striking copper-colored foliage. The leaves of the Copper Beech have been used in traditional herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to their rich content of tannins and flavonoids, which possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Regular consumption of Copper Beech has been associated with several health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved cardiovascular health, and enhanced cognitive function. Copper Beech can be prepared as a tea or tincture, often in combination with other herbs, and is sometimes used in traditional folk medicine to promote overall well-being and vitality.
